questions.text: Who is the 'Truly Beautiful Mind' referring to in this chapter?
questions.explanation: The chapter is a short biography of Albert Einstein, highlighting his scientific genius and humanistic outlook.

questions.text: When was Albert Einstein born?
questions.explanation: Albert Einstein was born on March 14, 1879, in the German city of Ulm.

questions.text: What did Einstein's mother think of him when he was a child?
questions.explanation: To his mother, Albert was a freak because his head seemed much too large.

questions.text: At what age did Einstein finally start speaking?
questions.explanation: Einstein was a late talker and didn't start speaking until he was two and a half years old.

questions.text: What was Einstein's playmates' nickname for him?
questions.explanation: He didn't know what to do with other children, so they called him 'Brother Boring'.

questions.text: What kind of toys did Einstein especially love as a child?
questions.explanation: He showed an early interest in the mechanics of things, looking even at his newborn sister as a mechanical toy.

questions.text: What did Einstein's headmaster once tell his father?
options: He will never make a success at anything
questions.explanation: The headmaster had a very low opinion of Albert's future prospects.

questions.text: At his mother's insistence, Einstein learned to play which instrument?
questions.explanation: He became a gifted amateur violinist, maintaining this skill throughout his life.

questions.text: Why did Einstein leave the school in Munich for good?
questions.explanation: He felt stifled by the strict discipline and often clashed with teachers.

questions.text: In which country did Einstein wish to continue his education because it was more liberal?
questions.explanation: He moved to German-speaking Switzerland, which was more open-minded than Munich.

questions.text: Who was the fellow student Einstein fell in love with at the university in Zurich?
questions.explanation: Mileva was a Serbian student whom Einstein found to be a 'clever creature'.

questions.text: Einstein called his desk drawer at the patent office the '_________'.
questions.explanation: He was secretly developing his own ideas while supposedly auditing others' inventions.

questions.text: Einstein's Special Theory of Relativity (1905) gave the famous formula:
questions.explanation: This formula describes the relationship between mass and energy.

questions.text: Einstein's General Theory of Relativity (1915) provided a new interpretation of:
questions.explanation: It was proved correct by an eclipse of the sun in 1919.

questions.text: In which year did Einstein receive the Nobel Prize for Physics?
questions.explanation: He was awarded the Nobel Prize in 1921 for his services to Theoretical Physics.

questions.text: Why did Einstein emigrate to the United States in 1933?
options: Because the Nazis came to power in Germany
questions.explanation: As a Jew, he fled the rise of the Nazi regime.

questions.text: Einstein wrote a letter to the American President warning about the atomic bomb. Who was the President?
questions.explanation: The letter was dated August 2, 1939.

questions.text: How did Einstein react to the atomic bombing of Hiroshima and Nagasaki?
questions.explanation: The destruction disturbed him so much that he proposed the formation of a world government.

questions.text: In his later years, Einstein used his popularity to campaign for:
questions.explanation: He spent his later years advocating for the end of the arms build-up.

questions.text: When Einstein died in 1955, he was celebrated as a:
questions.explanation: He was recognized as much for his humanity as for his scientific genius.

questions.text: Where was the 'German city' Einstein was born in?
questions.explanation: He was born in Ulm, though the family soon moved to Munich.

questions.text: What was the name of Einstein's sister?
questions.explanation: The story mentions his sister Maja when he was looking for wheels on her.

questions.text: At what age did the family move to Munich?
questions.explanation: They moved when he was 15 months old.

questions.text: Einstein was good at which subject in school?
questions.explanation: He was highly gifted in mathematics and interested in physics.

questions.text: Why did Einstein's mother oppose his marriage to Mileva at first?
options: Mileva was three years older than Albert
questions.explanation: She also thought Mileva was 'a book' like Albert, meaning too intellectual.

questions.text: In which year did Einstein finally marry Mileva?
questions.explanation: They married in 1903 and had two sons.

questions.text: Einstein divorced Mileva in 1919 and married his cousin:
questions.explanation: He married Elsa the same year he divorced Mileva.

questions.text: What was Einstein's job in 1902?
options: Technical expert in a patent office
questions.explanation: He worked at the patent office in Bern.

questions.text: Newspapers proclaimed Einstein's work as a '_________'.
questions.explanation: His 1919 success with the eclipse led to this headline.

questions.text: Einstein wrote a public missive to the _________.
questions.explanation: He proposed a world government in this missive to the UN.

questions.text: What does the word 'philistines' mean in the text?
options: People who do not like art, music or literature
questions.explanation: Einstein was at odds with the 'philistines' in his family and at university.

questions.text: Einstein was a 'humanist'. This means he believed in:
questions.explanation: His focus on peace and world government showed his humanistic side.

questions.text: Where did Einstein go to university?
questions.explanation: He studied at the University of Zurich after leaving school.

questions.text: The solar eclipse that proved Einstein's theory happened in:
questions.explanation: The 1919 eclipse confirmed his General Theory of Relativity.

questions.text: In 'E = mc²', 'c' stands for:
options: Speed of light in a vacuum
questions.explanation: It is a fundamental constant in physics (~300,000 km/s).

questions.text: How old was Einstein when he died?
questions.explanation: He was born in 1879 and died in 1955 (1955-1879=76).

questions.text: What does 'unraveling' mean in the context of Einstein's marriage?
questions.explanation: His private life (marriage) was unraveling (failing) as he became famous.

questions.text: What was the discovery in Berlin that made American physicists upset?
questions.explanation: German scientists discovered nuclear fission, leading to fears of a Nazi atomic bomb.

questions.text: Einstein is known as a 'citizen of the _______'.
questions.explanation: He was recognized as a 'world citizen' due to his global outlook and work for peace.

questions.text: The title 'A Truly Beautiful Mind' refers to:
options: The combination of his genius and his humanity
questions.explanation: It celebrates his intellectual beauty as well as his beautiful heart for humanity.
